macro_summary: U.S. equities staged a broad rebound on Monday, led by a 6.7% surge in semiconductor stocks (SOXX) that recovered most of last week's losses. The Nasdaq jumped 2.3% and the S&P 500 added 0.6%, helped by reports that Iran would halt attacks on Israel and a partial easing of Middle East tensions. WTI crude fell 3.2% to $90.56, but the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed and Houthi rebels renewed their Red Sea blockade. Treasury yields held near 4.55% as traders braced for the May CPI report on Wednesday and the possibility of a Fed rate hike this year.
commodities_note: WTI fell 3.2% to $90.56 on tentative de-escalation hopes, but the Hormuz crisis enters its 98th day with no durable resolution. Gold was flat at $4,355.70. The DXY slipped 0.1% to 99.95.

The BIS tightening of export controls on Chinese subsidiaries (per Reuters, Jun 9) introduces a near-term headline risk. An entry would become attractive on a test of the 50-day at $203.97 with volume; R:R is currently only 0.7:1 to nearest resistance. Patience wins here.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains under NIM pressure with the US10Y still far above the 4.20% reset level and no MAS easing until October. DBS and OCBC have both extended above their 50-day averages and are now on CAUTION, while UOB remains the only name still on WATCH — trading at a material discount to the cluster. The intra-cluster divergence favors UOB as the value play, though negative revenue growth tempers conviction.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally under pressure — awaiting MAS easing
singapore.key_development: US10Y at 4.55% keeps USD funding costs elevated; the earliest relief signal remains the October MAS decision.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster staged a furious 6.7% rebound (SOXX) from last week's AVGO-driven rout, but the recovery is uneven. NVDA reclaimed its 50-day and remains the only mega-cap name on WATCH with a compelling valuation discount. LITE and GOOG crossed back above their averages, while foundry names (TSM, INTC, MU) extended further and remain hard-blocked. The cluster is now polarized: the few names approaching entry zones (NVDA, LITE) are balanced by a dozen extreme-extension CAUTIONs.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — polarization widening
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX rallied 6.7% as Iran-Israel de-escalation hopes and a Google-Intel foundry leak (per Reuters, Jun 8) boosted chipmakers; the CPI report tomorrow is the next catalyst.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is bifurcated: MSFT and GOOG are near their 50-day moving averages and are on WATCH, with MSFT's NHS Copilot deal and GOOG's TPU momentum providing fundamental support. AMZN and PLTR remain below their averages and are in a holding pattern, with PLTR's distorted risk/reward keeping it from being actionable. The cluster's aggregate valuation is reasonable, but individual entry gates are still gated on price reconnection with the 50-day.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's 505,000-staff NHS Copilot deployment (per Simply Wall St., Jun 9) is the largest real-world AI healthcare rollout, while GOOG benefits from a reported Intel foundry deal for custom TPUs.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) make it a direct beneficiary of the same hyperscaler ASIC spend that drives AVGO — the S&P 500 inclusion news (per WSJ, Jun 8) is a sentiment catalyst.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Marvell pulls back 8-10% from its S&P 500 inclusion spike and consolidates near the 50-day.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Received a matching $2B NVDA photonics investment alongside LITE; any LITE catalyst (earnings, NVDA deal) directly reads through to COHR’s optical transceiver revenue.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor ahead of LITE’s next earnings — a strong print would lift COHR’s valuation, making a pullback to its 50-day a potential entry.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier after SK Hynix and Micron; Samsung’s qualification status with NVDA is the key read-through on memory supply diversity and the durability of the HBM pricing cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung’s HBM3E qualification with NVDA is confirmed by a major news wire — currently speculative.

interconnected.name: Corning
interconnected.reason: Signed a multi-billion optical fiber and cable agreement with Amazon for U.S. data center infrastructure (per StockStory, Jun 8); directly tied to AMZN’s AI capex cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Track post-deal consolidation — a pullback to the 50-day after the initial spike would offer a risk/reward entry if AMZN’s capex trajectory holds.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed (day 98) — Iran signals a halt to attacks on Israel but the blockade persists; Houthi rebels renew Red Sea blockade against Israeli-linked shipping.

US-China tech tensions escalate — BIS tightens export controls on overseas Chinese subsidiaries of chipmakers (Reuters, Jun 9); Pentagon adds Alibaba, BYD, Baidu to its military-company blacklist (SCMP, Jun 9).
ECB set to raise rates this week despite eurozone Q1 GDP contraction — risks a policy error reminiscent of 2011 (Bloomberg, Jun 8); could strengthen the euro and weigh on US equities.
Fed rate hike odds climb after strong May jobs data — 10Y yield at 4.55%; CPI on Wednesday is the next critical data point (Bloomberg/KITCO, Jun 8).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil has eased to $90.56, but the Hormuz stalemate continues to put a floor under energy costs, compressing margins for SG banks and keeping the Fed hawkish. The chip rebound on Monday suggests markets are pricing a de-escalation scenario, but the CPI print could quickly reassert rate fears. ASML and TSM face direct headline risk from US-China export tightening while PLTR and defense-adjacent names benefit from elevated geopolitical tension.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran signaled a halt to direct attacks on Israel; Houthis renewed their Red Sea blockade. BIS issued new licensing requirements for exports to Chinese subsidiaries abroad.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds with WTI in the $85–95 range; Fed stays on hold until at least September. Semis consolidate their rebound, but extension blocks keep most names on CAUTION. SG banks continue to suffer from elevated US10Y; OCBC is the relative value pick but entry requires a break below 4.20%.

optimistic.description: A ceasefire in the Middle East lifts the oil risk premium, sending WTI below $80; CPI comes in soft, easing rate-hike fears. Semis rally further, with NVDA and LITE upgrading to actionable. SG bank NIMs benefit as the 10Y drops toward 4.20%.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz closure worsens or a direct US-Iran confrontation causes WTI to spike above $100; CPI surprises to the upside, prompting the Fed to signal a near-term hike. Semis sell off again; all current CAUTION names remain blocked, and WATCH names slide back to their 50-day lines.

wildcard.description: A major US-China tech rupture — e.g., full export ban on advanced chips — hits ASML, TSM, and NVDA severely. The semis cluster enters a structural downturn independent of macro, with even discounted valuations insufficient to attract buyers.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain a cash-heavy stance; only add on pullbacks to the 50-day in names with clear catalysts after the CPI print; avoid all extreme-extension semis regardless of valuation.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — selectively watching a handful of near-entry names amid broad extension
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 12 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the semis rebound has not cleared the overbought condition. A hawkish CPI print tomorrow could quickly reprice rate expectations and invalidate the fragile WATCH setups.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: Vera Rubin samples confirmed shipping; optics integration revealed in Rubin rack design. Blackwell Ultra → Rubin transition timeline given.
Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

watch_for_entry.note: 0.8% above 50-day after NHS Copilot win; entry on a dip below $410 with stop at $398.58 and upside to $424. R:R 1.0:1 sizing, so small starter only if CPI passes quietly.
2.2% above 50-day; a pullback to $353.24 with volume would create a clean 5.4:1 R:R to $404. TPU momentum and Intel foundry deal provide catalyst.
Reclaimed 50-day, but 0.9% stop makes headline R:R useless; sizing R:R 0.7:1. A deeper dip toward $850 would offer a much better entry. Watch only.
Near resistance with poor R:R; wait for a dip to $37.32 support or 50-day at $36.95 for a reasonable entry.
2.3% above 50-day; a test of $203.97 with volume would be actionable. BIS export control overhang keeps it on watch.

new_stocks_to_watch.note: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO; S&P 500 inclusion catalyst. Add to watchlist if the stock pulls back 8-10% from its post-announcement spike and consolidates near its 50-day.
NVDA photonics partner alongside LITE. Monitor ahead of LITE’s next earnings — a strong read would make a pullback to COHR’s 50-day an attractive entry.
Third HBM supplier; watch for NVDA HBM3E qualification news as a trigger. The stock is already extended, but a confirmation of qualification could justify a re-rating.
Amazon’s multi-billion optical fiber deal (per StockStory, Jun 8) ties it directly to AMZN’s capex cycle. Add on a pullback to the 50-day after the initial spike.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Core CPI comes in at or below 0.3% MoM, easing rate-hike fears and pushing the 10Y below 4.50%.
bullish_upgrades: NVDA → BUY candidate if 50-day test holds
MSFT → BUY candidate if dip below $410 confirms
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI surprises to the upside (0.5%+ MoM), driving the 10Y above 4.60% and cementing a near-term rate hike.
bearish_impact: All WATCH names revert to HOLD as 50-day tests fail

bullish_upgrades: Semis cluster could see SOXX break above 600 again, lifting WATCH names
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Fed hikes or signals an imminent hike, with a hawkish dot plot projecting further increases.
bearish_impact: All CAUTION names remain blocked; WATCH names slip back below 50-day
AVGO, AMZN, CRWV face further multiple compression

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
